description: String rendered on the web home page. Flip via the admin API to prove the feature-flag pipeline (Sprint 0 acceptance criterion).
Whether the home page shows the live API health/version line. Demonstrates a typed (bool) feature flag.
Days an unverified account keeps full use (incl. rating) after signup (ADR-011 soft verification). Past this, rating requires a verified email.
Minimum days between handle changes (Sprint 2 spec).
Ratings in a category before its profile counts as 'warming'. Below this the feed leans on popularity and shows no match percentages.
Ratings before full profile confidence (Sprint 3 spec's min-5, as a tier). Drives the 'needs N more ratings' prompt and feed shape.
Nightly full palate-profile recompute (heals drift after catalog vector changes; per-rating recompute covers the rest).
UTC hour for the nightly palate recompute (9 = ~3am US Central).
Nearest-neighbor candidate pool per category for the sectioned feed.
Pool rank where Up Your Alley ends and Stretch a Little begins.
Pool rank where Stretch a Little ends and the Wildcard sample space begins.
Items per feed section at warm/full confidence.
Items per Alley/Stretch section for cold users (confidence-adaptive shape).
Wildcard picks for full-confidence users.
Wildcard picks for cold/warm users — exploration teaches the engine fastest early.
Weight (percent) of the smoothed popularity prior blended into candidate scores.
MMR-lite relevance/diversity balance (percent toward relevance).
Cross-category bridge recs (ADR-027, the moat mechanic). Flag exists for rollout control only — the capability is a required deliverable.
Bridge picks injected per (source, target) category pair.

description: Onboarding staples quiz, beer (JSON; editable without deploy). Entries resolve by producer+name; unresolved entries drop out.

value: [{"producer":"Buffalo Trace Distillery","name":"Buffalo Trace Kentucky Straight Bourbon"},{"producer":"Maker's Mark Distillery","name":"Maker's Mark"},{"producer":"Jack Daniel's Distillery","name":"Jack Daniel's Old No. 7"},{"producer":"Jameson Distillery","name":"Jameson Irish Whiskey"},{"producer":"Glenfiddich Distillery","name":"Glenfiddich 12 Year"},{"producer":"Laphroaig Distillery","name":"Laphroaig 10 Year"},{"producer":"Templeton Distillery","name":"Templeton Rye 4 Year"},{"producer":"Woodford Reserve Distillery","name":"Woodford Reserve"}]
description: Onboarding staples quiz, whiskey (JSON; editable without deploy).
Onboarding staples quiz, wine BY VARIETAL (style codes; each resolves to a representative catalog drink so quiz ratings stay real ratings).
Confidence (percent, 0-100) assigned to drink attribute values inherited from style baselines (ADR-009). Vector sync reads this.
Minimum pg_trgm name similarity (percent) for a producer pair to enter the merge queue.
Minimum pg_trgm name similarity (percent) for a same-category drink pair to enter the merge queue.
Nightly user-user CF batch that rebuilds the palate-match graph (ADR-007/014).
UTC hour for the nightly match rebuild (after the palate recompute at 9 so it reads fresh profiles).
Minimum drinks two users must both have rated in a category before their co-rating Pearson counts at all (ADR-007). Below this the match is pure attribute similarity.
Significance-weighting constant: co-rating Pearson is scaled by n/(n+shrinkage), damping correlations from thin overlaps.
Density weight constant for the blend: CF weight = coRated/(coRated+K). Larger K keeps attribute similarity dominant longer as density grows (ADR-014/025).
Neighbor edges materialized per user in the nightly batch (strongest blended scores).
Match-% display: 'eager' shows a percent from day one (labeled 'early estimate' at low confidence); 'conservative' shows it only at med+ confidence. Ships eager per charter (ADR-006/014).
Co-rated drinks (best category) for a match to reach 'med' confidence.
Co-rated drinks (best category) for a match to reach 'high' confidence.
Matches below this blended percent are not shown.
Minimum rating for a match's drink to count as a 'recent love' (social proof + feed).
How many of the strongest matches feed the 'Loved by Your Matches' section and rec social proof.
Matches returned on the 'Your Matches' surface.
Items in the 'Loved by Your Matches' feed section.
Weight (percent) of the CF match signal blended into Up Your Alley ranking as co-rating density grows (hybrid recs, ADR-014). Density-gated: no effect for cold users.

Master switch for sending the weekly digest. Stays OFF until an SMTP provider AND a CAN-SPAM physical address are configured (HUMAN-CHECKLIST 6). While off, the digest is composed and LOGGED, never delivered.
UTC hour the weekly digest job runs (14 = ~8am US Central).
Day of week the weekly digest runs (0=Sunday … 6=Saturday; 1=Monday).
CAN-SPAM footer mailing address. BLANK until a founder-provided registered-agent/virtual address exists (HUMAN-CHECKLIST 6/15). Empty forces the digest log-only, even if digest.enabled is true.

Absolute base URL for links in the digest (the background job has no HTTP request to derive host from). Unsubscribe link is {base}/api/digest/unsubscribe?token=… — under /api/*, which the service worker leaves as a full-page navigation.
Digest block: this week's rating recap (breadth framing only, never volume).
Digest block: weekly-distinct-drink discovery streak (ADR-016 — never consumption volume/frequency).
Digest block: top recommendation per feed section.
Digest block: a palate-match hook ('a strong palate match was found' / 'your match tried X').
Cards per page on the rapid-rate browse surface (server default when the client omits pageSize).
Default confidence (percent, 0-100) for editorial attribute overrides in product-seed files (source='moderator', ADR-028) when the file declares no attributeConfidence of its own.
Hours a check-in stays active before it expires (ADR-015 session window; founder ruling 2026-07-10). Checking in elsewhere ends the previous one immediately.
Wiki rate limit: venues one account may add per rolling 24h (Sprint 5 spec).
Wiki rate limit: menu-item adds/edits/confirms one account may make per rolling 24h (Sprint 5 spec).
Minimum pg_trgm name similarity (percent) for a public-venue pair to enter the merge queue (mirrors the producer threshold).
Geo gate for venue dedupe candidates: name-similar pairs only queue when within this many meters (pairs where either side lacks geo still queue on name alone).
Minimum latest rating for a menu drink to shelve as a Favorite (four-shelf mapping, founder ruling 2026-07-10).
Of unrated, unfamiliar menu drinks ranked by palate similarity, the closest X percent shelve as New for You; the rest as Adventurous.
Nightly full badge evaluation (Sprint 6). Inline hooks award instantly; the nightly heals misses and catches streak thresholds crossing without a write.
UTC hour the nightly badge evaluation runs.
Provenance weighting (Sprint 6): public aggregates + CF only count ratings from accounts at least this many days old. Own profile unaffected.
Provenance weighting (Sprint 6): public aggregates + CF only count ratings from accounts with at least this many latest ratings. Own profile unaffected.
Write-abuse limit: rating writes one account may make per rolling hour (Sprint 6 hardening; bot defense, never consumption limiting). 0 disables.
Write-abuse limit: reports one account may file per rolling 24h (Sprint 6). 0 disables.
Nightly anomaly scan (Sprint 6): flags z-score rating outliers and rapid-fire write bursts for HUMAN review. Detection only — never an automatic action.
UTC hour the nightly anomaly scan runs.
Absolute z-score of a user's mean deviation from drink averages at which an outlier flag opens.
Minimum latest public ratings a user needs before the z-score detector considers them (small samples are noise).
Sliding-window size for the rapid-fire detector.
Rating writes within the window that open a rapid-fire flag (bot/script patterns; last 24h scanned).
Show the custom PWA install prompt (Android/desktop beforeinstallprompt UI + iOS add-to-home-screen hint; Sprint 6).
